reasoning: On April 14, 2025, Starlink was granted a 10-year official license to operate in Lesotho. Furthermore, the company was permitted to operate as a 100% foreign-owned entity, bypassing local equity requirements which often slow down implementation (as has been the case in other African markets where local joint-ventures or partners were mandated). The critical hurdle — regulatory approval — has already been definitively cleared. Looking at similar situations across Africa, the time from license approval to official ‘Availability’ on the Starlink map is typically between 1 and 3 months. The delay to July 1 represents roughly 2.5 months (78 days from license approval). Given Starlink's expanding satellite coverage and operations in neighboring Eswatini, Botswana, Zimbabwe, and Mozambique, ground infrastructure and regional logistics are already mature. Therefore, there is a high likelihood the service will be turned on and listed as ‘Available’ on the official map prior to July 1, 2025.
